title: Notes from the Storm
synopsis: When impossible purple snow begins falling inside the school gymnasium right before the big game, Ivy and her classmates exchange notes to investigate the mystery, discovering that working together reveals more clues than anyone could find alone.

pages.text:
Purple snow is falling from the gymnasium ceiling at 3:02 PM. I am Ivy, lead investigator of impossible weather.

Observation 1: The flakes are not cold. They feel warm like summer rain but look like tiny purple feathers.

Observation 2: Coach Martinez keeps looking up and scratching his head. He has never seen indoor snow before either.

Observation 3: The basketball hoops are getting decorated with purple dust. This might make shooting harder.

I need more data. Will pass notes to other students for their observations.

- Investigator Ivy

illustration.description: A girl with short dark brown bob with bangs, fair skin, big brown eyes writes in a notebook while sitting on gymnasium bleachers. Purple snowflakes drift down around her in the bright school gymnasium. Basketball equipment and colorful banners are visible. The art style features bright sunny playground colors, primary colors, white space, joyful energetic atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Ivy writing field notes while purple snow falls in the gym

pages.text:
Dear Ivy,

I saw where the purple snow started! It came from the air vent near the scoreboard. But that's weird because air vents blow air OUT, not IN.

Also, my sneakers are turning purple where I step. The snow sticks to rubber.

- Marcus (sitting by the basketball cart)

---

Dear Marcus,

Excellent clue! I will examine the air vent.

New discovery: The purple snow tastes like grape popsicles. I licked some off my hand. Don't tell Coach Martinez.

Question: Are you willing to help me solve this mystery?

- Ivy

---

Dear Ivy,

YES! This is the coolest thing that ever happened during PE.

- Marcus

illustration.description: A girl with short dark brown bob with bangs, fair skin, big brown eyes and a boy with dark skin pass a folded note between them in a bright gymnasium. Purple snow continues falling around diverse children who are pointing up at the ceiling and collecting snowflakes. Basketball equipment is covered in purple dust. The art style features bright sunny playground colors, primary colors, white space, joyful energetic atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Ivy and Marcus passing investigation notes while other kids watch the purple snow

pages.text:
MYSTERY SOLVING TEAM - OFFICIAL NOTES

From: Zoe (by the water fountain)
To: Ivy and Marcus

I heard you need more investigators! I found something important. The janitor's closet door is open and there are purple footprints leading FROM it TO the air vent. But they're not human footprints. They have three toes.

---

From: Ivy
To: Marcus and Zoe

Three toes! What creature has three toes? We need to follow this trail.

Marcus: Check the supply closet.
Zoe: Watch the air vent.
Me: I'm going to argue with whatever is making this mess.

---

From: Marcus
To: Team

Found it! There's a tiny purple bird in the supply closet. It's shivering and has three toes. I think it's lost.

illustration.description: Three children huddle together sharing notes in a bright gymnasium filled with purple snow. The girl with short dark brown bob with bangs, fair skin, big brown eyes (Ivy) stands in the center, with Marcus (a boy with dark skin) and Zoe (a girl with curly red hair) on either side. Purple three-toed footprints are visible on the gymnasium floor. Other diverse children play in the background. The art style features bright sunny playground colors, primary colors, white space, joyful energetic atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Ivy, Marcus, and Zoe sharing investigation notes while following purple footprints

pages.text:
FINAL INVESTIGATION REPORT

From: The Mystery Team (Ivy, Marcus, Zoe)
To: Everyone

We solved it! The purple snow was coming from a tiny lost weather bird. Weather birds are responsible for making snow in their home dimension, but this one fell through a portal into our gymnasium.

The bird was scared and trying to call for help by making familiar snow. But in our world, its snow magic turned purple and warm instead of white and cold.

Zoe suggested we help it find the portal home. Marcus found the shimmery spot by the basketball net where reality looked wobbly.

I argued with the portal until it opened wider. (Sometimes being argumentative helps!)

The weather bird chirped thank-you and flew home. The purple snow stopped immediately.

Case Status: SOLVED
Method: Teamwork
New Discovery: We make a pretty good investigation squad.

Want to solve mysteries together again tomorrow?

- Team Weather Detectives

illustration.description: A girl with short dark brown bob with bangs, fair skin, big brown eyes stands with Marcus and Zoe watching a tiny purple bird with three toes fly through a shimmery portal near a basketball hoop. The purple snow has stopped falling and the bright gymnasium is returning to normal. Diverse children cheer in the background as the mystery is solved. The art style features bright sunny playground colors, primary colors, white space, joyful energetic atmosphere.
illustration.alt: The Weather Detectives team watching the purple bird return home through a portal

Basketball hoops and colorful banners are visible. Other diverse children are scattered around, looking up in wonder at the impossible indoor snow. The art style features bright sunny playground colors, primary colors, white space, joyful energetic atmosphere.
coverPrompt.alt: Ivy and classmates in gymnasium with purple snow falling indoors

questions.question: What color was the impossible snow falling in the gymnasium?
questions.explanation: The purple snow was the main mystery that Ivy and her friends investigated throughout the story.

questions.question: How did the purple snow feel when Ivy touched it?
questions.explanation: Ivy noted in her field report that the purple flakes felt warm like summer rain, which was part of what made them so mysterious.

questions.question: What did Marcus discover about where the snow was coming from?
questions.explanation: Marcus observed that the purple snow was coming from the air vent near the scoreboard, which was an important clue.

questions.question: What kind of footprints did Zoe find?
questions.explanation: Zoe discovered purple three-toed footprints leading from the janitor's closet to the air vent, which helped them find the weather bird.

questions.question: How did the team solve the mystery?
questions.explanation: The Weather Detectives solved the mystery by sharing observations, exchanging notes, and working as a team to help the lost weather bird.
